mnemonics: EARS: Entity sets Are Relationships' Subjects. Remember that an entity set is a collection of similar entities (like a table of customers), and they participate in relationships with defined constraints.
keyPoints: An entity set is a collection of entities of the same type (e.g., all Customers).
Structural constraints define the rules for relationship participation: cardinality ratio (one-to-one, one-to-many, many-to-many) and participation constraint (total vs. partial).

Cardinality restricts the number of entities another entity can be associated with, while participation specifies if an entity's existence requires a relationship (e.g., every order must have a customer = total participation).
commonMistakes: Confusing entity sets with relationships: an entity set is a 'thing' (Student), while a relationship is an association between things (Enrolls_In).
Mixing up cardinality constraints: thinking a many-to-many relationship can be directly implemented in a relational schema without a junction table, which is not the case.
